description: One position / role in a work history — the canonical, version-independent record of a job. Resume documents (is.dame.resume) reference these by AT URI and choose which highlights to surface, so the same job can be reused and tailored across many resumes without duplicating its facts.

main.description: Record key is a human-readable slug (e.g. "ipfs-content-manager") so backlinks from resumes read clearly and re-imports are idempotent.

title.description: Role title held at this organization.

startDate.description: Partial date, "YYYY" or "YYYY-MM". Day-level precision is unnecessary for a resume.

endDate.description: Same format as startDate. Omit (and set current=true) for an ongoing role.

current.description: True when this is an ongoing role. Renders as "– Present".

summary.description: Optional one-line framing of the role, shown above its highlights.

highlights.description: Achievement bullets. Each carries a stable local id so a resume can select, reorder, and reuse a subset ("tailoring") without copying text.

skills.description: Skills exercised in this role. Aggregated across selected jobs to build a resume's skill list.

links.description: Portfolio pieces and external links tied to this role — the work you did here. Each carries a stable id so a resume can select, reorder, and reuse a subset per version (mirrors highlights). Rendered under the role.

highlight.description: A single achievement / responsibility bullet, individually addressable within its job.

id.description: Stable id, unique within this job. Referenced from is.dame.resume entries[].highlightIds.

text.description: The canonical phrasing — what a resume shows when it selects this bullet without naming a variant.

visibility.description: Display intent for the website. NOT a privacy control — every PDS record is publicly fetchable. "private" bullets are never rendered on the site; "unlisted" render only when a resume explicitly selects them.

featured.description: Emphasis flag — surfaced first / styled as a lead achievement.

metric.description: Marks a quantified, outcome-bearing bullet (useful for impact-first resumes).

tags.description: Free-form labels (e.g. "growth", "leadership", "writing") used to filter/tailor which bullets a given resume pulls in.

variants.description: Forked phrasings of this same bullet — the one achievement re-framed for different audiences. A resume selects a variant with a "<highlightId>#<variantId>" ref in entries[].highlightIds; selecting the bare highlight id shows the canonical text. Variants share the parent's flags (visibility, featured, metric, tags).

highlightVariant.description: One alternate phrasing of a highlight. Lives on the canonical job record so a fork is edited once and every resume that selected it stays in sync.

id.description: Stable id, unique within the parent highlight (e.g. "v1"). Addressed from resumes as "<highlightId>#<id>".

label.description: What this phrasing is for, e.g. "design-focused" or "one-pager" — shown in the admin variant picker, never rendered on the site.

link.description: A portfolio piece or external link tied to a job — a sample of the work done in that role, rendered under it. Point it at a first-party /creating post with `work` (resolved to that post's title, URL, and cover as an embed) or at any external page with `url`.

id.description: Stable id, unique within this job. Referenced from is.dame.resume entries[].linkIds.

work.description: Backlink to a first-party portfolio post (a site.standard.document homed in the portfolio publication, shown at /creating/<slug>). Resolved live, so the embed tracks the post's current title and cover. Takes precedence over `url`.

url.description: External URL for a piece that isn't a /creating post. Used only when `work` is absent.

label.description: Display label. For a `work` link it overrides the resolved post title; for a `url` link it is the link text.

description.description: Optional one-line note shown beneath the link.

visibility.description: Site display intent (NOT privacy). "private" links never render; "unlisted" render only when a resume explicitly selects them.
